title: Tao Te Ching
author: Laozi
description: A short philosophical classic about the nature of the way, balance, and non-forcing.

title: The Book of Tea
author: Kakuzo Okakura
description: Philosophical reflections on beauty, impermanence, and presence through the lens of Japanese tea ceremony.

title: The Natural History of Selborne
author: Gilbert White
description: Careful observations of wildlife and seasonal change in an English village, written as letters.

title: Frankenstein
author: Mary Shelley
description: A created being searches for understanding, connection, and meaning in a world that did not ask for its existence.

title: Flatland
author: Edwin Abbott Abbott
description: A mathematical allegory about a two-dimensional world encountering dimensions beyond its perception.

title: Meditations
author: Marcus Aurelius
description: Private reflections on Stoic philosophy — how to think, act, and rest with integrity.

title: A Classical Dictionary
author: John Lemprière
description: A comprehensive guide to the figures, places, and myths of the ancient world — gods, heroes, and histories from Greece and Rome.

title: Alice's Adventures in Wonderland
author: Lewis Carroll
description: A child falls into a world of impossible logic and absurd order, where sense and nonsense trade places.

title: An Enquiry Concerning Human Understanding
author: David Hume
description: A rigorous examination of how we know what we think we know — causation, belief, and the limits of reason.

title: Aspects of Nature (Vol. 1)
author: Alexander von Humboldt
description: Vivid scientific and literary explorations of the natural world, from tropical forests to the night sky — nature as interconnected whole.

title: Bulfinch's Mythology
author: Thomas Bulfinch
description: A generous retelling of Greek, Roman, and Norse myths — gods, monsters, and the stories that shaped Western imagination.

title: Leaves of Grass
author: Walt Whitman
description: An expansive, democratic celebration of the self, the body, nature, and belonging — poetry as breath and presence.

title: Poems
author: Emily Dickinson
description: Compressed, slant-rhymed meditations on death, consciousness, nature, and eternity — enormous questions in small rooms.

title: The Golden Bough
author: James George Frazer
description: A sweeping comparative study of myth, magic, and ritual across cultures — tracing the deep patterns beneath human belief.

title: The Principles of Science
author: William Stanley Jevons
description: A systematic investigation of scientific method, logic, and inference — how knowledge is built and how it can fail.

title: The Problems of Philosophy
author: Bertrand Russell
description: A lucid introduction to the core puzzles of philosophical inquiry — perception, matter, truth, and what we can claim to know.

title: The Song Celestial
author: Edwin Arnold (trans.)
description: A poetic translation of the Bhagavad-Gita — duty, action, selflessness, and the nature of the soul in dialogue between warrior and god.

title: The Time Machine
author: H.G. Wells
description: A traveller leaps far into the future and finds humanity divided, diminished, and ultimately lost to entropy.
